revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nabeta Cave Tombs]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nabeta Cave Tombs | native_name = 鍋田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = Nabeta Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nabeta Cave Tombs | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tombs | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|01|13|N|130|40|16|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = cave tomb cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|01|13|N|130|40|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nabeta Cave Tombs'''|鍋田横穴| Nabeta yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a cluster of late [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that many of these tombs are [[decorated kofun]] with geometric and figurative patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2803 |title= 鍋田横穴|trans-title=Nabeta Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nabeta Cave Tombs are located on a 500-meter stretch of cliffs of [[:en:Mount Aso|Aso]] [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] on the west bank of the Iwano River, a tributary of the Kikuchi River, in northern Kumamoto Prefecture. A total of 61 cave tombs have been identified, of which 16 are decorated. Tomb No. 27 is particularly noteworthy, with a double decorative border, a rectangular tomb chamber 2 meters deep and 2.5 meters wide, and a flat floor. On the left outer wall are a figure with arms and legs outstretched, a sword, a bow, an octopus-shaped quiver with six arrowheads, a knives, and a small bow with arrows nocked, and a shield. Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. There are many other cave groups scattered throughout Yamaga city, such as the Nagaiwa Cave Tombs and the Shiro Cave Tombs. Currently, the surrounding area has been developed as Nabeta Recreation Forest Park.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.yamaga.kumamoto.jp/kiji00393/index.html Yamaga city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Nabeta Cave Tombs'''|鍋田横穴| Nabeta yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a cluster of late [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that many of these tombs are [[decorated kofun]] with geometric and figurative patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2803 |title= 鍋田横穴|trans-title=Nabeta Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nabeta Cave Tombs are located on a 500-meter stretch of cliffs of [[:en:Mount Aso|Aso]] [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] on the west bank of the Iwano River, a tributary of the Kikuchi River, in northern Kumamoto Prefecture. A total of 61 cave tombs have been identified, of which 16 are decorated. Tomb No. 27 is particularly noteworthy, with a double decorative border, a rectangular tomb chamber 2 meters deep and 2.5 meters wide, and a flat floor. On the left outer wall are a figure with arms and legs outstretched, a sword, a bow, an octopus-shaped quiver with six arrowheads, a knives, and a small bow with arrows nocked, and a shield. Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. There are many other cave groups scattered throughout Yamaga city, such as the Nagaiwa Cave Tombs and the Shiro Cave Tombs. Currently, the surrounding area has been developed as Nabeta Recreation Forest Park.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nabeta Cave Tombs]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nabeta Cave Tombs | native_name = 鍋田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = Nabeta Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nabeta Cave Tombs | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tombs | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|01|13|N|130|40|16|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = cave tomb cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|01|13|N|130|40|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nabeta Cave Tombs'''|鍋田横穴| Nabeta yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a cluster of late [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Yamaga, Kumamoto|Yamaga, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that many of these tombs are [[decorated kofun]] with geometric and figurative patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2803 |title= 鍋田横穴|trans-title=Nabeta Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nabeta Cave Tombs are located on a 500-meter stretch of cliffs of [[:en:Mount Aso|Aso]] [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] on the west bank of the Iwano River, a tributary of the Kikuchi River, in northern Kumamoto Prefecture. A total of 61 cave tombs have been identified, of which 16 are decorated. Tomb No. 27 is particularly noteworthy, with a double decorative border, a rectangular tomb chamber 2 meters deep and 2.5 meters wide, and a flat floor. On the left outer wall are a figure with arms and legs outstretched, a sword, a bow, an octopus-shaped quiver with six arrowheads, a knives, and a small bow with arrows nocked, and a shield. Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. There are many other cave groups scattered throughout Yamaga city, such as the Nagaiwa Cave Tombs and the Shiro Cave Tombs. Currently, the surrounding area has been developed as Nabeta Recreation Forest Park.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2803 |title= 鍋田横穴|trans-title=Nabeta Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nabeta Cave Tombs are located on a 500-meter stretch of cliffs of [[:en:Mount Aso|Aso]] [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] on the west bank of the Iwano River, a tributary of the Kikuchi River, in northern Kumamoto Prefecture. A total of 61 cave tombs have been identified, of which 16 are decorated. Tomb No. 27 is particularly noteworthy, with a double decorative border, a rectangular tomb chamber 2 meters deep and 2.5 meters wide, and a flat floor. On the left outer wall are a figure with arms and legs outstretched, a sword, a bow, an octopus-shaped quiver with six arrowheads, a knives, and a small bow with arrows nocked, and a shield. Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. There are many other cave groups scattered throughout Yamaga city, such as the Nagaiwa Cave Tombs and the Shiro Cave Tombs. Currently, the surrounding area has been developed as Nabeta Recreation Forest Park.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>

On the left outer wall are a figure with arms and legs outstretched, a sword, a bow, an octopus-shaped quiver with six arrowheads, a knives, and a small bow with arrows nocked, and a shield. Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. There are many other cave groups scattered throughout Yamaga city, such as the Nagaiwa Cave Tombs and the Shiro Cave Tombs. Currently, the surrounding area has been developed as Nabeta Recreation Forest Park.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>

A total of 61 cave tombs have been identified, of which 16 are decorated. Tomb No. 27 is particularly noteworthy, with a double decorative border, a rectangular tomb chamber 2 meters deep and 2.5 meters wide, and a flat floor. On the left outer wall are a figure with arms and legs outstretched, a sword, a bow, an octopus-shaped quiver with six arrowheads, a knives, and a small bow with arrows nocked, and a shield. revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Nabeta Cave Tombs]] to [[Nabeta Yokoanabos]]: Text replacement - "Cave Tomb" to "Yokoanabo"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nabeta Cave Tombs]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nabeta Cave Tombs]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nabeta Cave Tombs]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nabeta Cave Tombs | native_name = 鍋田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = Nabeta Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nabeta Cave Tombs | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tombs | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|01|13|N|130|40|16|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = cave tomb cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|01|13|N|130|40|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nabeta Cave Tombs'''|鍋田横穴| Nabeta yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} containing a cluster of late [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave Tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is significant in that many of these tombs are [[decorated kofun]] with geometric and figurative patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2803 |title= 鍋田横穴|trans-title=Nabeta Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nabeta Cave Tombs are located on a 500-meter stretch of cliffs of {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Aso}} {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} on the west bank of the Iwano River, a tributary of the Kikuchi River, in northern Kumamoto Prefecture. A total of 61 cave tombs have been identified, of which 16 are decorated. Tomb No. 27 is particularly noteworthy, with a double decorative border, a rectangular tomb chamber 2 meters deep and 2.5 meters wide, and a flat floor. On the left outer wall are a figure with arms and legs outstretched, a sword, a bow, an octopus-shaped quiver with six arrowheads, a knives, and a small bow with arrows nocked, and a shield. Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. There are many other cave groups scattered throughout Yamaga city, such as the Nagaiwa Cave Tombs and the Shiro Cave Tombs. Currently, the surrounding area has been developed as Nabeta Recreation Forest Park.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.yamaga.kumamoto.jp/kiji00393/index.html Yamaga city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nabeta Yokoanabos]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nabeta Yokoanabos]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nabeta Yokoanabos]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nabeta Yokoanabos | native_name = 鍋田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = Nabeta Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nabeta Yokoanabos | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Yokoanabos | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|01|13|N|130|40|16|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga, Kumamoto|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = cave tomb cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|01|13|N|130|40|16|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Nabeta Yokoanabos'''|鍋田横穴| Nabeta yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing a cluster of late [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|Yamaga, Kumamoto|WD=Q861214|lt=Yamaga, Kumamoto|1=Yamaga, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is significant in that many of these tombs are [[decorated kofun]] with geometric and figurative patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2803 |title= 鍋田横穴|trans-title=Nabeta Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nabeta Yokoanabos are located on a 500-meter stretch of cliffs of {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|WD=Q733710|lt=Aso|1=Mount Aso}} {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} on the west bank of the Iwano River, a tributary of the Kikuchi River, in northern Kumamoto Prefecture. A total of 61 cave tombs have been identified, of which 16 are decorated. Tomb No. 27 is particularly noteworthy, with a double decorative border, a rectangular tomb chamber 2 meters deep and 2.5 meters wide, and a flat floor. On the left outer wall are a figure with arms and legs outstretched, a sword, a bow, an octopus-shaped quiver with six arrowheads, a knives, and a small bow with arrows nocked, and a shield. Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. There are many other cave groups scattered throughout Yamaga city, such as the Nagaiwa Yokoanabos and the Shiro Yokoanabos. Currently, the surrounding area has been developed as Nabeta Recreation Forest Park.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|WD=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

A total of 61 cave tombs have been identified, of which 16 are decorated. Tomb No. 27 is particularly noteworthy, with a double decorative border, a rectangular tomb chamber 2 meters deep and 2.5 meters wide, and a flat floor. Beneath the large and small quivers is a horse carved in relief with its head facing the passage. There are many other cave groups scattered throughout Yamaga city, such as the Nagaiwa Yokoanabos and the Shiro Yokoanabos. Currently, the surrounding area has been developed as Nabeta Recreation Forest Park.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
